You overhear a fragment of a conversation between two powerful mages and an
alchemist about a potion made with an herb called vervain, and the rarity
of this herb.

You catch the end of a tale spun by an old story teller which tells of a
battle between a mighty demon and a warrior wielding a magical axe.

A hooded stranger standing a few feet from you hurriedly speaks to a nearby
companion in hushed tones about the fact that red wizards have been forging
weapons of a magical nature of late.

You overhear a veteran adventurer admonishing a traveling companion for his
hasty use of a newly purchased heartstone, before understanding its power.

An elven warrior sitting at the table next to yours comments to a companion
about the neccessity of carefully examining subterranean areas for recondite
passages.

An elderly wizard comments to an apprentice to be careful when casting high
level non-combat spells during combat, the outcome may not be favorable.

You overhear a master of arms telling some warriors that they might want
to stop by the magic shop to take some potions with them on their next
adventure, should it prove to be of great length.

A dwarven warrior sitting next to you at the bar comments about a quest
for the High Priest, who needs some type of rare herb that only grows
deep underground.

While sitting at the bar, you overhear the end of a story told by a gnome
to some young adventurers that tells about a giant that breathes flame, but
at the end of the story the gnome says it is only a myth.

A tired old dwarven warrior starts speaking to whomever will listen to what
he has to say about an underground complex which is under the control of
red wizards who summon very powerful creatures to guard their abode.
A human hunter speaks of a large town that is just across the great lake.

You overhear an elderly wizard giving an apprentice a lecture on the names
of various magickal creatures in the language of spellcraft. He then tells
the young man that there is a very powerful beast called an 'arok' dwelling
deep below the mountains.

After a small bag of coins changes hands at a table nearby, you overhear the lithe young man accepting payment tell his associate in hushed tones, "In the hall of the dwarven kings, speak the password 'dwarflord' to enter".
